summary: Bug-fix verification (iteration 2): force-seed customer preservation, forgot-password flows (existing + ghost user), admin password-reset-link, live SMTP delivery logs, and frontend ForgotPassword spam-notice + home page regression. ALL 4 backend tests PASS (4/4). ALL frontend checks PASS. Customer password restored to Customer@123 at end of test run.

issues: React warning: 'React does not recognize the offsetDistance prop on a DOM element.' Non-blocking, likely from a motion / animation library forwarding props to a DOM node. Filter the prop or wrap in motion component to silence.

action_items: No blocking actions. (Optional) Silence the React 'offsetDistance' prop warning in console.
Open items still pending from iteration_1: GET /api/airports returns <200, ProtectedRoute race on /account hard-refresh, PNR font ambiguity (O/0).
critical_code_review_comments: forgot_password endpoint correctly handles both branches (existing user -> send + log password_reset, ghost -> log password_reset_no_user/skipped). Status 200 always returned -- no enumeration leak.

admin_seed(force=true) now only deletes the four demo emails (admin@/pilot@/crew@/customer@aerovista.com) instead of wiping the whole users collection. Verified by registering a fresh customer pre-force-seed and confirming login still works post-force-seed.
admin/password-reset-link endpoint requires admin role and returns reset_link, user, name, expires_minutes. Token is a 60-min JWT with purpose='reset' -- usable by /auth/reset-password.

Real SMTP path verified: email_logs row for password_reset to customer@aerovista.com has status='sent', error_message='', sent_at populated. multipart/alternative + List-Unsubscribe / Message-ID headers per emails.py.
/app/backend/server.py is now 1059 lines -- still recommend splitting into routers (auth, admin, bookings, flights, etc.) for maintainability.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@aerovista.com/Admin@123; Customer: customer@aerovista.com/Customer@123 (RESTORED back to Customer@123 at end of the reset-password test).
seed_data_creation: Test registers a throwaway preserve-test-<rand>@example.com customer (used by the force-seed preservation test) and generates ghost-user-<rand>@example.com email_logs rows. No cleanup required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 bug fixes for password-reset-after-force-seed regression PASS. New focused test file: /app/backend/tests/test_password_reset_and_seed.py (4 tests, ~9s). The pre-existing /app/backend/tests/test_aerovista.py from iteration 1 was NOT re-run this iteration (per main-agent instructions to focus on bug-fix scope). Customer password was reset to NewPass@123 then restored to Customer@123 within the same test -- /app/memory/test_credentials.md remains accurate.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: previous force-seed wiped db.users entirely so a real customer registering, then somebody calling /api/admin/seed?force=true, would silently destroy their account. forgot-password then returned 200 (anti-enumeration) but did NOT send an email -- hence the user's complaint. Fix verified: force-seed now only deletes the 4 demo emails; forgot-password additionally writes a 'skipped' email_logs row when no user matches, giving ops a paper trail. The earlier 'aerovista gmail' messages the user saw were unrelated Sent-folder copies from previous SMTP smoke tests.
